attacks_attempted.payload: empty hotelName, checkout before checkin, oversized optional strings
<script>alert(1)</script> in hotel/notes fields
call create/update/cancel with no write permission
create accommodation in event A using registrationId from another event/person
call create action directly for a person with no active travel record
create/update/cancel accommodation and inspect side effects for audit write
create/update/cancel accommodation and inspect side effects for cascade emission
concurrent update/cancel after stale read with unchanged row id but changed state
large rooming metadata strings within and above schema limits

bugs_found.description: createAccommodationRecord accepted arbitrary registration IDs, allowing an accommodation row in one event to be linked to a registration from a different event or person.
bugs_found.fix: Validated registrationId with eventId + personId scoping before insert.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/accommodation.test.ts > rejects registration IDs from another event/person

bugs_found.description: The travel-first business rule only existed in the form picker, so direct server-action calls could create accommodation rows for people without any active travel record.
bugs_found.fix: Added a server-side active-travel check before accommodation creation.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/accommodation.test.ts > rejects creating accommodation without a non-cancelled travel record

bugs_found.description: Accommodation create, update, and cancel mutations completed without writing the required audit log entry or dispatching their cascade event, bypassing downstream notifications and flagging.
bugs_found.fix: Wired create/update/cancel through writeAudit and emitCascadeEvent with accommodation-specific payloads; update emits only when trigger fields change.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/accommodation.test.ts > writes an audit log and emits a cascade event on create

bugs_found.description: updateAccommodationRecord and cancelAccommodationRecord fetched the row under event scope but wrote back by id only, so a concurrent status change could still be overwritten after the read.
bugs_found.fix: Added compare-and-swap style update filters using eventId, non-cancelled status, and updatedAt when available; stale writes now fail loudly.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/accommodation.test.ts > blocks concurrent updates that changed the record after it was read

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid with otherwise valid attendance query input
route eventId for event A with body eventId for event B
attendance row scoped to event A joined to registration metadata by registrationId without event-scoping the joined registration table
concurrent check-in between total, by-method, and by-session aggregate SELECTs under default Postgres READ COMMITTED transaction isolation
date/sessionId query payloads containing SQL metacharacters
read attendance as read-only user and attempt write escalation through this read-only module
oversized/unknown query fields and unbounded attendance list request
<script>alert(1)</script> in returned person/registration fields

bugs_found.description: Attendance read actions validated the body eventId after assertEventAccess and did not validate getConfirmedRegistrationCount eventId at all, so malformed route IDs could reach auth/database queries before Zod rejected them.
bugs_found.fix: Added route eventId validation before auth/database access for all attendance read actions.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/attendance.test.ts > rejects a malformed route eventId before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: listAttendanceRecords joined event_registrations by registrationId only. A bad or legacy attendance row in the active event could surface registrationNumber/category metadata from another event because the joined table was not scoped to eventId.
bugs_found.fix: Scoped the registration left join with eventRegistrations.eventId = active eventId and rejected body/route eventId mismatches.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/attendance.test.ts > event-scopes joined registration metadata to prevent cross-tenant leakage

bugs_found.description: getAttendanceStats performed three aggregate SELECTs in a default transaction. Under Postgres READ COMMITTED, concurrent check-ins can appear between SELECTs and return a total that disagrees with by-method/by-session counts.
bugs_found.fix: Requested repeatable-read isolation for the stats transaction so all aggregates use one stable snapshot.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/attendance.test.ts > uses repeatable-read isolation so concurrent check-ins cannot tear aggregate snapshots

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid with otherwise valid offline batch body
route eventId for event A with body eventId for event B
offline record with sessionId from another event while QR token belongs to the active event
QR payload eventId from another event
call processBatchSync without write permission
double-submit the same offline scan concurrently
oversized batch, QR payload, and deviceId values
SQL metacharacters inside QR payload and deviceId
<script>alert(1)</script> in QR payload and deviceId
force registration/person/insert database errors

bugs_found.description: processBatchSync called assertEventAccess before validating the route eventId, so malformed route IDs reached auth work and then returned an item-level invalid result instead of rejecting the request.
bugs_found.fix: Validate the route eventId with the offline sync eventId schema before auth or database work.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/batch-sync.test.ts > rejects a malformed route eventId before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: processBatchSync parsed input.eventId but never compared it to the route eventId, allowing stale or cross-event bodies to be accepted under a different route scope.
bugs_found.fix: Reject body/route eventId mismatches before auth and use the validated scoped eventId for all QR comparisons, registration lookup, deterministic IDs, and inserts.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/batch-sync.test.ts > rejects body and route eventId mismatches before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: Offline sync accepted any UUID-shaped sessionId and inserted it into an attendance row for the active event without verifying that the session belonged to that event.
bugs_found.fix: Validate non-null sessionId values with an event-scoped sessions lookup before registration lookup or attendance insert.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/batch-sync.test.ts > rejects a sessionId that is not scoped to the active event

attacks_attempted.payload: call updateEventBranding with logoStorageKey/headerImageStorageKey pointing at branding/{OTHER_EVENT_ID}/... then call getBrandingImageUrls
call uploadBrandingImage with runtime imageType '../../header'
upload image/svg+xml containing <script> and onload
upload file declared image/png whose bytes are plain text
read event.branding, concurrently update the same event, then save stale merged JSON
call update/upload/delete branding with read-only access
upload files over 5MB and submit max-length branding text fields
SQL metacharacters in branding text fields and storage key strings
__proto__ and constructor.prototype fields in updateEventBranding input
invalid eventId, invalid colors, wrong MIME type, empty file, malformed storage keys
oversized sender/footer/prefix strings and control/unicode payloads
missing event, invalid MIME, stale update, and missing file error paths

bugs_found.description: updateEventBranding accepted arbitrary logo/header storage keys, and getBrandingImageUrls signed whatever was stored. A write-capable user could point one event at another event's R2 branding object and receive a signed URL for it.
bugs_found.fix: Validate direct storage-key updates against branding/{eventId}/{logo|header}/, sanitize legacy stored branding before returning it, and refuse to sign out-of-scope keys.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/branding.test.ts > rejects direct cross-event logo storage keys before updating; does not sign stored image keys outside the active event scope

bugs_found.description: uploadBrandingImage trusted the TypeScript-only imageType union at runtime. A direct caller could pass '../../header', producing a logical storage key outside the intended logo/header prefix.
bugs_found.fix: Validate imageType at runtime before auth, storage, or database work.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/branding.test.ts > rejects runtime-invalid image types before auth or storage work

bugs_found.description: Branding upload trusted file.type and allowed active SVG or spoofed image content to be stored and later rendered/signed as event branding.
bugs_found.fix: Validate PNG/JPEG/WebP magic bytes and reject active SVG constructs including script tags, event-handler attributes, javascript: URLs, foreignObject, iframe, object, and embed.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/branding.test.ts > rejects spoofed PNG uploads whose bytes are not PNG data; rejects active SVG payloads before upload

bugs_found.description: updateEventBranding read the current branding JSON, merged a partial update, then wrote the full JSON object by event id only. Concurrent updates could silently clobber each other.
bugs_found.fix: Select events.updatedAt and use a compare-and-swap update filter with returning(); stale writes now fail before cache revalidation.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/branding.test.ts > blocks stale branding saves that race with another update
